superseded_sources.held: external, not committed to this repository

superseded_sources.name: v2 whitepaper/README
superseded_sources.held: external, not committed to this repository

directives.title: Humanity Before Technology
directives.heading: ## Directive 1 — Humanity Before Technology
directives.statement: Whenever technology and human freedom conflict, freedom wins; whenever profit conflicts with the Constitution, the Constitution wins.

directives.title: Assume Every Central Authority Will Eventually Fail
directives.heading: ## Directive 2 — Assume Every Central Authority Will Eventually Fail
directives.statement: Every authority, dependency, server, and maintainer must be assumed temporary, compromisable, or mortal; removing any one of them must never destroy Mininet.

directives.title: The Network Must Outlive Its Creators
directives.heading: ## Directive 3 — The Network Must Outlive Its Creators
directives.statement: The protocol must keep functioning without anyone's permission, even if every current contributor disappeared tomorrow.

directives.title: Money Is Never Worth More Than Legitimacy
directives.heading: ## Directive 4 — Money Is Never Worth More Than Legitimacy
directives.statement: The ledger must always answer "who owns what" with certainty; two honest nodes reconciling to different answers means the design is wrong.

directives.title: Canonical Truth Is Sacred
directives.heading: ## Directive 5 — Canonical Truth Is Sacred
directives.statement: Everything else may merge or disagree, but there is exactly one canonical settlement history, and ownership changes only through canonical consensus, never through offline-invented alternative truth.

directives.title: Design for Failure, Not Success
directives.heading: ## Directive 6 — Design for Failure, Not Success
directives.statement: Assume every kind of outage, disaster, and compromise will eventually happen; the protocol must degrade gracefully and never panic.

directives.title: Forks Must Remain Free, But Legitimacy Must Be Earned
directives.heading: ## Directive 7 — Forks Must Remain Free, But Legitimacy Must Be Earned
directives.statement: Anyone may fork the code, but a fork inherits no humanity, trust, or continuity — legitimacy is defined by continuous adherence to the Constitution and the verified-human community, not by a repository or trademark.

directives.title: The Human Is the Root of Trust
directives.heading: ## Directive 8 — The Human Is the Root of Trust
directives.statement: Consensus, governance, economics, and recovery all trace back to verified humans, never governments, corporations, validators, token holders, or founders; anything drifting toward institutional trust must be removed.

directives.title: Privacy Is Structural
directives.heading: ## Directive 9 — Privacy Is Structural
directives.statement: Privacy must come from mathematics, never from promises, policies, or ethics — a system that depends on trust to preserve privacy is already broken.

directives.title: Every Optimization Has a Cost
directives.heading: ## Directive 10 — Every Optimization Has a Cost
directives.statement: Every shortcut trades something away (memory, security, decentralization, auditability, predictability, human equality); if the trade can't be clearly explained, reject it.

directives.title: The Weakest Device Matters Most
directives.heading: ## Directive 11 — The Weakest Device Matters Most
directives.statement: Engineer for the old phone in a village, not the billionaire's server — a network that serves only powerful hardware serves only powerful people.

directives.title: AI Serves Humanity
directives.heading: ## Directive 12 — AI Serves Humanity
directives.statement: AI may propose and discover; only humans decide and legitimize. No model, regardless of capability, may become a source of authority.

directives.title: Think in Centuries
directives.heading: ## Directive 13 — Think in Centuries
directives.statement: Optimize for one hundred years, not the next quarter — choose the solution that survives longer whenever uncertain.

directives.title: Simplicity Is Security
directives.heading: ## Directive 14 — Simplicity Is Security
directives.statement: Every line of code, protocol rule, and dependency is a liability; the strongest protocol is usually the one that removed the most unnecessary parts.

directives.title: Build for the Honest Majority, Defend Against the Malicious Minority
directives.heading: ## Directive 15 — Build for the Honest Majority, Defend Against the Malicious Minority
directives.statement: Welcome those who come to build without trusting those who come to exploit; security should come from incentives, cryptography, and transparency, not suspicion of everyone.

directives.title: Preserve the Voice/Value Wall at All Costs
directives.heading: ## Directive 16 — Preserve the Voice/Value Wall at All Costs
directives.statement: Money may buy storage, computation, bandwidth, and attention, but never governance — directly, indirectly, or accidentally; any proposal creating even a subtle path from wealth to political power must be rejected.

directives.title: Every Decision Must Survive the "Future Child" Test
directives.heading: ## Directive 17 — Every Decision Must Survive the "Future Child" Test
directives.statement: Before every major decision, ask whether it makes a child born a century from now, who chose none of this and never knew the founders, more or less free — if less, the decision is wrong.

directives.title: The Edge May Be Convenient; The Core May Never Depend On It
directives.heading: ## Directive 18 — The Edge May Be Convenient; The Core May Never Depend On It
directives.statement: The edge (banks, carriers, couriers, states, vendors, courts) may be convenient but the core may never depend on it — every provider must be replaceable and switchable by one human alone, and the core must survive the total disappearance of all of them.
